mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth exceeds 2% YoY but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines but 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of cases, or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.3pp but 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) during low-rate environments (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates stagflation risks, depressing equity returns as central banks prioritize inflation control over growth.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1% but 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity, increasing corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises > 1% MoM but 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST < 700k) signals weak demand for construction, reducing related sector revenues and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST < 700k but 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or signal IC < 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise > 1% QoQ but 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) create uncertainty, reducing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL > 2% but 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.
